pi_items.source: Task complexity in verifier.py (requires multiple wall creations, minimum 35 furniture items across 6 categories, label placement, and 3D photo rendering).
Explicitly defined in verifier.py header and matches the task description narrative.
Explicitly coded in verifier.py: `passed = score >= 70` and documented in the scoring header.
Explicitly coded and documented in verifier.py scoring (C1=20, C2=25, C3=20, C4=20, C5=15).
Explicitly defined as STARTER_DST in setup_task.sh and checked via export_result.sh as the baseline SH3D_FILE.
Explicitly defined in verifier.py header, and verifier logic actively scores these features (furniture_count, new_walls, new_labels, photo_found).

privileged_info_summary: I verified that the task operates in a synthetic SweetHome3D environment where the starting shell ('photography_studio_starter.sh3d') is procedurally generated by stripping all furniture from an existing sample ('SweetHome3DExample.sh3d'). The verifier strictly scores the agent on generating at least 3 new walls, 4 text labels, and 35 total pieces of furniture (including specific counts for desks, chairs, shelves, lamps, sofas, and decor), as well as rendering a 3D photo to the desktop. All metadata claims exactly match the programmatic constraints in setup_task.sh and verifier.py.
